title: Sour Flaky Peruvian Chicken Pollo A La Brasa
description: A sour and flaky one-pot Peruvian dish featuring muscovado sugar, this dairy-free-friendly Pollo A La Brasa is a twist on the classic recipe, using an ice-bath-shock to achieve tender and juicy chicken.
instructions: In a large pot, combine chicken thighs, muscovado sugar, soy sauce, lime juice, garlic, ginger, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Pour in vegetable oil and bring the mixture to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 30 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
Remove the pot from the heat and immediately submerge it in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Let the chicken cool to room temperature, then refrigerate it for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Remove the chicken from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Bake the chicken in the preheated oven for 20 minutes or until it is crispy and golden brown.
Remove the chicken from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before serving.
